risk_factors: Multiple admissions (4)
care_plan_summary: Schedule a follow-up appointment within 7 days, Phone check-in within 24 hours by the nursing team

follow_up: Schedule a follow-up appointment within 7 days
Phone check-in within 24 hours by the nursing team
Home health nurse visit within 48 hours
Arrange cardiology/endocrinology review if applicable
medication: Do not skip doses. Set daily reminders.
Do not stop or change medications without consulting the doctor.
Bring all medications to your follow-up appointment.
monitoring: General Monitoring: Monitor general health and report any unusual symptoms immediately.
Temperature monitoring twice daily — report fever above 38°C.
Weight monitoring daily — report sudden weight gain or loss.
Maintain a symptom diary to track any changes.
dietary: Heart Health: Reduce salt and saturated fat intake.
Eat balanced meals with fruits, vegetables, and whole grains.
Choose lean proteins (fish, chicken, legumes).
Limit sugar, processed foods, and unhealthy fats.
Drink at least 8 glasses (2 liters) of water daily.
exercise: Light physical activity for 30 minutes daily (e.g., walking, light stretching).
Start slowly and gradually increase activity levels.
Walk with assistance or walking aids.
Physiotherapy sessions as scheduled.
Avoid strenuous activity until cleared by the doctor.
lifestyle: Sleep: Get 7-8 hours of quality sleep nightly.
Alcohol: Avoid alcohol consumption completely.
Smoking: Quit smoking immediately — seek support if needed.
Stress: Practice stress management (deep breathing, meditation).
emergency: URGENT: Visit the nearest hospital immediately if you experience:
Chest pain or severe shortness of breath
Severe dizziness or fainting
Difficulty speaking or weakness on one side of the body
Uncontrolled bleeding or severe pain
Emergency Contact: Call Suwa Setha Hospital Hotline at +94 11 234 5678 (24/7)
support: Support: Ensure family or caregiver availability for daily assistance.
Keep a health diary to track symptoms, medications, and appointments.
Keep emergency contacts readily accessible.

risk_factors: Advanced age (>65), Heart disease, COPD, Extended stay (>7 days)
care_plan_summary: Schedule a follow-up appointment within 14 days, Phone check-in within 72 hours

follow_up: Schedule a follow-up appointment within 14 days
Phone check-in within 72 hours
Monitor symptoms and report any changes
medication: Do not skip doses. Set daily reminders.
Do not stop or change medications without consulting the doctor.
Bring all medications to your follow-up appointment.
monitoring: Cardiac Monitoring: Monitor for chest pain, shortness of breath, or palpitations. Keep a symptom diary.
Respiratory Monitoring: Monitor oxygen saturation using a pulse oximeter twice daily.
Temperature monitoring twice daily — report fever above 38°C.
Weight monitoring daily — report sudden weight gain or loss.
Maintain a symptom diary to track any changes.
dietary: Heart Health: Reduce salt and saturated fat intake.
Eat balanced meals with fruits, vegetables, and whole grains.
Choose lean proteins (fish, chicken, legumes).
Limit sugar, processed foods, and unhealthy fats.
Drink at least 8 glasses (2 liters) of water daily.
Avoid saturated fats and trans fats.
exercise: Bed-based exercises as advised by the physiotherapist.
Change position every 2 hours to prevent bedsores.
Passive range of motion exercises for joints.
Avoid strenuous activity until cleared by the doctor.
lifestyle: Sleep: Get 7-8 hours of quality sleep nightly.
Alcohol: Avoid alcohol consumption completely.
Smoking: Quit smoking immediately — seek support if needed.
Stress: Practice stress management (deep breathing, meditation).
emergency: Contact your general practitioner if you experience any unusual symptoms.
Visit the nearest clinic if symptoms worsen.
Emergency Contact: Suwa Setha Hospital Hotline: +94 11 234 5678
support: Support: Ensure family or caregiver availability for daily assistance.
Keep a health diary to track symptoms, medications, and appointments.
Keep emergency contacts readily accessible.

risk_factors: Diabetes, COPD, Extended stay (>7 days), Poor recovery status
care_plan_summary: Schedule a follow-up appointment within 7 days, Phone check-in within 24 hours by the nursing team

follow_up: Schedule a follow-up appointment within 7 days
Phone check-in within 24 hours by the nursing team
Home health nurse visit within 48 hours
Arrange cardiology/endocrinology review if applicable
medication: Do not skip doses. Set daily reminders.
Do not stop or change medications without consulting the doctor.
Bring all medications to your follow-up appointment.
monitoring: Diabetes Monitoring: Check blood glucose levels twice daily (morning and evening). Maintain a log.
Respiratory Monitoring: Monitor oxygen saturation using a pulse oximeter twice daily.
Temperature monitoring twice daily — report fever above 38°C.
Weight monitoring daily — report sudden weight gain or loss.
Maintain a symptom diary to track any changes.
dietary: Heart Health: Reduce salt and saturated fat intake.
Eat balanced meals with fruits, vegetables, and whole grains.
Choose lean proteins (fish, chicken, legumes).
Limit sugar, processed foods, and unhealthy fats.
Drink at least 8 glasses (2 liters) of water daily.
Diabetes Management: Follow diabetic meal plan — monitor carbohydrate intake.
Avoid sugary drinks and high-GI foods.
exercise: Bed-based exercises as advised by the physiotherapist.
Change position every 2 hours to prevent bedsores.
Passive range of motion exercises for joints.
Avoid strenuous activity until cleared by the doctor.
lifestyle: Sleep: Get 7-8 hours of quality sleep nightly.
Alcohol: Avoid alcohol consumption completely.
Smoking: Quit smoking immediately — seek support if needed.
Stress: Practice stress management (deep breathing, meditation).
emergency: URGENT: Visit the nearest hospital immediately if you experience:
Chest pain or severe shortness of breath
Severe dizziness or fainting
Difficulty speaking or weakness on one side of the body
Uncontrolled bleeding or severe pain
Emergency Contact: Call Suwa Setha Hospital Hotline at +94 11 234 5678 (24/7)
support: Support: Ensure family or caregiver availability for daily assistance.

risk_factors: No significant risk factors identified.
care_plan_summary: Schedule a follow-up appointment within 14 days, Phone check-in within 72 hours

follow_up: Schedule a follow-up appointment within 14 days
Phone check-in within 72 hours
Monitor symptoms and report any changes
medication: Do not skip doses. Set daily reminders.
Do not stop or change medications without consulting the doctor.
Bring all medications to your follow-up appointment.
monitoring: General Monitoring: Monitor general health and report any unusual symptoms immediately.
Temperature monitoring twice daily — report fever above 38°C.
Weight monitoring daily — report sudden weight gain or loss.
Maintain a symptom diary to track any changes.
dietary: Heart Health: Reduce salt and saturated fat intake.
Eat balanced meals with fruits, vegetables, and whole grains.
Choose lean proteins (fish, chicken, legumes).
Limit sugar, processed foods, and unhealthy fats.
Drink at least 8 glasses (2 liters) of water daily.
exercise: Light physical activity for 30 minutes daily (e.g., walking, light stretching).
Start slowly and gradually increase activity levels.
Walk with assistance or walking aids.
Physiotherapy sessions as scheduled.
Avoid strenuous activity until cleared by the doctor.
lifestyle: Sleep: Get 7-8 hours of quality sleep nightly.
Alcohol: Avoid alcohol consumption completely.
Smoking: Quit smoking immediately — seek support if needed.
Stress: Practice stress management (deep breathing, meditation).
emergency: Contact your general practitioner if you experience any unusual symptoms.
Visit the nearest clinic if symptoms worsen.
Emergency Contact: Suwa Setha Hospital Hotline: +94 11 234 5678
support: Support: Ensure family or caregiver availability for daily assistance.
Keep a health diary to track symptoms, medications, and appointments.
Keep emergency contacts readily accessible.

risk_factors: Heart disease, Hypertension, Extended stay (>7 days)
care_plan_summary: Schedule a routine follow-up appointment within 30 days, Regular monitoring as per standard protocol

follow_up: Schedule a routine follow-up appointment within 30 days
Regular monitoring as per standard protocol
medication: Do not skip doses. Set daily reminders.
Do not stop or change medications without consulting the doctor.
Bring all medications to your follow-up appointment.
monitoring: Blood Pressure: Monitor blood pressure daily. Record readings in a diary.
Cardiac Monitoring: Monitor for chest pain, shortness of breath, or palpitations. Keep a symptom diary.
Temperature monitoring twice daily — report fever above 38°C.
Weight monitoring daily — report sudden weight gain or loss.
Maintain a symptom diary to track any changes.
dietary: Heart Health: Reduce salt and saturated fat intake.
Eat balanced meals with fruits, vegetables, and whole grains.
Choose lean proteins (fish, chicken, legumes).
Limit sugar, processed foods, and unhealthy fats.
Drink at least 8 glasses (2 liters) of water daily.
Avoid saturated fats and trans fats.
exercise: Light physical activity for 30 minutes daily (e.g., walking, light stretching).
Start slowly and gradually increase activity levels.
Walk with assistance or walking aids.
Physiotherapy sessions as scheduled.
Avoid strenuous activity until cleared by the doctor.
lifestyle: Sleep: Get 7-8 hours of quality sleep nightly.
Alcohol: Avoid alcohol consumption completely.
Smoking: Quit smoking immediately — seek support if needed.
Stress: Practice stress management (deep breathing, meditation).
emergency: Contact your general practitioner if you experience any unusual symptoms.
Visit the nearest clinic if symptoms worsen.
